rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Build clique edges from pairwise comparison results

Source: R/comparison.R
comparison_to_edges.Rd

Converts output from summarize_comparison into the edge format expected by find_cliques and clique_persistence. Renames columns, injects species identity, and computes per-pair effect sizes and classification.

comparison_to_edges(
  comparison,
  sp1,
  sp2,
  alternative = c("greater", "less"),
  alpha = 0.05
)

Arguments

comparison

Data frame from summarize_comparison (the $results element). Must contain columns Species1, Species2, hog, plus q-value and effect-size columns from both directions.

sp1

Species abbreviation for Species1 genes (e.g., "BDIS").

sp2

Species abbreviation for Species2 genes.

alternative

Which test direction to use for q-values and classification: "greater" (conservation, default) or "less" (divergence).

alpha

Significance threshold for the type column (default 0.05).

Value

Data frame with columns:

gene1

Gene identifier (Species1)

gene2

Gene identifier (Species2)

species1

Species abbreviation for gene1 (sp1)

species2

Species abbreviation for gene2 (sp2)

hog

Ortholog group identifier

q.value

Minimum of the two directional q-values

effect_size

Geometric mean of directional effect sizes

type

"conserved" or "diverged" if q.value < alpha; "ns" otherwise

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
summary_AB <- summarize_comparison(comparison_AB)
edges_AB <- comparison_to_edges(summary_AB$results, "SP_A", "SP_B")

# Combine multiple species pairs for find_cliques()
edges <- rbind(edges_AB, edges_AC, edges_BC)
} # }
